usability_issues.issue: Missing interface purpose (界面目的) descriptions for the majority of the screens.
usability_issues.suggestion: Define clear goals and purposes for each screen to guide the design and development process.

usability_issues.issue: Inappropriate and repetitive button variants lacking visual hierarchy. S001 uses Filled Buttons for all actions, S005 uses Outlined Buttons for all actions. 'AI reply' is incorrectly listed as a button.
usability_issues.suggestion: Establish clear visual hierarchy using Filled for primary, Outlined for secondary, and Text for tertiary actions. Remove system responses like 'AI reply' from user action lists.

usability_issues.screen_or_section: S003 AI对话页 & S005 自由对话页
usability_issues.issue: Generic empty state (Illustration + CTA) is inappropriate for a conversational interface.
usability_issues.suggestion: Use a conversational greeting, AI avatar introduction, or suggested starter prompts as the empty state for chat screens.

usability_issues.issue: Both 'Approve' and 'Reject' actions are defined as Filled Buttons, which could lead to accidental destructive actions.
usability_issues.suggestion: Use a Filled Button for the primary action (Approve) and an Outlined or Text Button with an error/warning color for the destructive action (Reject).

missing_patterns.pattern: Chat Interface & Voice Visualization (Message bubbles, typing indicators, mic recording waveforms)
missing_patterns.context: S003 AI对话页 and S005 自由对话页

missing_patterns.pattern: Flashcard Interactions (Tap to flip, swipe gestures for spaced repetition)
Audio Playback & Comparison Controls (Play/pause, timeline, waveform visualization)
Visual Node/Script Editor UI (Drag-and-drop, branching logic visualization)

strengths: Strong foundation in Material Design 3 with well-defined color tokens, typography scales, and component specifications.
Clear distinction in layout patterns between Consumer (Bottom navigation/Single column) and Professional (Navigation rail/Side nav) roles.
Consistent inclusion of key UI states (Empty, Loading, Error, Success) across all screens to ensure robust error handling and feedback.

inconsistencies.issue: All are list-style screens with similar action counts but belong to different user contexts (learning vs admin vs gamification) and likely require different visual density and hierarchy treatments.
Both are conversation interfaces under the same module but have different action counts, suggesting different interaction complexity that may require distinct layouts rather than a shared template.
Both are purchase-related screens with similar low action counts but may require differentiated emphasis (subscription vs one-time purchase) instead of identical templates.
Both are data dashboards in different modules (admin vs user-facing) and likely require different data density, navigation, and visualization treatments.

token_gaps.context: Text/icon color on success backgrounds (e.g., achievement badges, success alerts).
Text/icon color on warning backgrounds (e.g., expiring subscription, risk alerts).
Informational state color for neutral system messages and tips.
Color definitions for disabled buttons, inputs, and navigation items.
Hover state tokens for web (MUI) components.
Focus ring color and outline token for accessibility compliance.
Multiple elevation/shadow tiers for dialogs, bottom sheets, app bars beyond a single shadow token.
Consistent spacing system for padding/margins across dense dashboards and relaxed learning screens.
